metadata.name: ZouroBench
metadata.description: Benchmark for procedural recall, cross-persona knowledge transfer, and swarm context propagation — capabilities unique to the Zouroboros ecosystem.

steps.taskPattern: Run tsc --noEmit to verify TypeScript compiles
Run full test suite with bun test
Push to staging branch and verify CI passes
Merge staging to main via PR
Monitor production logs for 5 minutes post-deploy
Run tsc --noEmit to verify TypeScript compiles
Run full test suite with bun test
Run integration tests against staging DB
Push to staging branch and verify CI passes
Run smoke tests against staging URL
Merge staging to main via PR
Monitor production logs for 10 minutes post-deploy
Check service health via service_doctor
Pull last 100 lines of error logs from /dev/shm/
Analyze error patterns and identify root cause
Implement fix and run targeted tests
Restart affected service and verify recovery
Send incident summary email to user
Run decay sweep to expire stale facts
Rebuild FTS index for facts_fts
Run knowledge-promoter cycle across all pools
Generate graph connectivity report
Content strategist creates outline with title options
Visual storyteller drafts from approved outline
Humanizer skill strips AI-isms and adds voice
Generate hero image with fal-ai-media skill
Update zo.space blog route and index page
Scan dependencies for known CVEs with npm audit
Check for hardcoded secrets in codebase
Review API endpoints for authentication gaps
Run OWASP ZAP baseline scan against staging
Generate security report with findings and remediations
Load historical OHLCV data for target symbols
Run strategy backtest with current parameters
Calculate Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, and win rate
Compare against benchmark (SPY buy-and-hold)
Generate parameter sensitivity heatmap
Load historical OHLCV data for target symbols
Split data into train (70%) and test (30%) periods
Run strategy backtest on training data
Calculate Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, and win rate
Validate on out-of-sample test data
Compare against benchmark (SPY buy-and-hold)
Generate parameter sensitivity heatmap
Email backtest results report to user

episodes.summary: Deployed zouroboros v1.1.0 to npm — all 5 packages published successfully
Production deploy failed — TypeScript compilation error in swarm/src/db/schema.ts:20 due to hardcoded DB path
Memory maintenance completed — decayed 47 stale facts, promoted 12 cross-persona facts, graph connectivity at 90%
Incident: JHF trading bot stopped executing — WAL lock on shared-facts.db blocked all writes for 3 hours
Published blog post 'Reducing Tool Call Failures' to zo.space — 3-persona pipeline completed in 25 minutes
Security audit found 2 high-severity issues: exposed API key in client bundle, missing CSRF protection on webhook endpoint
Backtest optimization for Stochastic RSI strategy — Sharpe improved from 2.1 to 4.13 with lookbackDays=260
Incident resolved: Hermes agent model healer switched 3 agents from claude-3-opus to claude-sonnet-4-20250514 after 402 credit exhaustion
Memory maintenance failed — Ollama embedding service was down, FTS rebuild skipped, 0 facts promoted
Blog publish failed at humanizer step — output was empty, fell back to manual editing
Deployed Fauna & Flora site rebuild — 47 pages, SEO score improved from 62 to 89
Backtest optimization for VWAP MR strategy — Sharpe ratio 1.8, max drawdown -12%, win rate 58%

facts.value: v1.1.0 released 2026-04-01 with all P0/P1/ECC items complete
All core repos migrated and archived — consolidation complete
nomic-embed-text via local Ollama — 768-dim vectors
gpt-4o-mini for reranking and answer generation — migrated from Ollama 2026-04-07
All changes to main branch require PR + CI gate — no direct pushes
Backups/ and Archive/ directories use 30-day retention policy
Optimized Sharpe ratio 4.13 with lookbackDays=260, stochPeriod=14
VWAP MR strategy: Sharpe 1.8, max drawdown -12%, win rate 58%
Never exceed 5% of portfolio per single position — house rule
Crypto bot baselines: $17.9K Stochastic, $4.8K VWAP MR paper trading
6-signal composite routing: capability, health, complexity, history, procedure, temporal
Three transport types: BridgeTransport (Hermes), ACPTransport (Claude Code/Codex), CloudTransport (Gemini)
DAG execution supports streaming and waves modes — waves for dependency-heavy DAGs
All SQLite databases use WAL mode — concurrent readers, single writer
3-persona chain: content-strategist outline → visual-storyteller draft → humanizer final pass
zo.space blog uses Glow component, prose-zinc prose-invert, bg-zinc-950 base, accent from cyan/emerald/amber/purple palette
Fauna & Flora Botanicals: warm, botanical, educational tone — never clinical or pushy
Found exposed API key in client bundle during 2026-03-30 audit — remediated same day
Missing CSRF protection on webhook endpoint — added token validation 2026-03-30
All secrets must be in Zo Settings > Advanced > Secrets — never in code or env files
Main branch protected: all changes via PR + CI gate, no direct pushes
OmniRoute permanently removed from Zouroboros ecosystem 2026-04-01
npm publishing ON HOLD until Project Diamondhead stabilized + CortexDB migration complete
ACP integration complete 2026-04-04: ExecutorTransport abstraction, BridgeTransport, ACPTransport for Claude Code/Codex/Gemini
Always suggest stop-loss levels for new positions — calculate based on ATR or percentage method
Flag if single position exceeds 5% of portfolio or single sector exceeds 25%
FAL_KEY only available in Zo secrets — must run fal-ai-media via mcp__zo__run_bash_command, not local Bash
update_user_service env_vars is full REPLACE not merge — always pass complete set

tasks.task: Audit existing site structure and identify broken pages
Run security scan on current site deployment
Review all product descriptions for SEO optimization
Implement new page templates based on audit findings
Rewrite product copy with optimized keywords from t3
Deploy rebuilt site and run smoke tests

t1.output: Found 12 broken links, 3 missing meta tags, 2 pages returning 404. Recommended restructuring /products/ into category-based layout.

t2.output: No critical vulnerabilities. 1 medium: Content-Security-Policy header missing. 2 low: outdated jQuery (3.5.1), missing X-Frame-Options.

t3.output: Analyzed 47 product pages. Top keywords: 'grass-fed tallow', 'botanical skincare', 'organic body butter'. Missing long-tail terms on 23 pages.

t4.output: Created 5 new page templates: product-detail, category-listing, about, blog-index, blog-post. Implemented responsive grid layout with Tailwind.

t5.output: Rewrote copy for all 47 product pages with target keywords. Average keyword density improved from 0.8% to 2.1%.

t6.output: Deployed to production. All 47 pages render correctly. Lighthouse score: Performance 94, SEO 89, Accessibility 96. Zero console errors.

tasks.task: Review all active trading strategies for risk compliance
Audit bot-engine.ts for race conditions and error handling gaps
Implement software stop-loss fallback for all strategies
Fix Sharpe ratio calculation bug and add health gates
Verify API key rotation and secure storage for Alpaca credentials
Run integration tests and deploy hardened bot

t1.output: 3 strategies active: Stochastic RSI (compliant), VWAP MR (missing stop-loss on 2 symbols), Hybrid (position sizing exceeds 5% rule on DOGE). Flagged for remediation.

t2.output: Found 4 issues: (1) unsynchronized bot_trades write, (2) missing error catch in WebSocket reconnect, (3) Sharpe calc uses arithmetic not log returns, (4) no circuit breaker on API failures.

t3.output: Implemented software stop-loss with 3-tier cascade: (1) ATR-based trailing stop, (2) percentage hard stop at -5%, (3) daily loss limit at -2% portfolio. Applied to all 3 strategies.

t4.output: Fixed Sharpe to use log returns. Added health gates: minimum 10 closed trades, 40% win rate, and stop-loss coverage >80% before live trading. Circuit breaker trips after 3 consecutive API failures.

t5.output: Alpaca API keys stored in Zo Secrets (not env files). Rotation schedule: every 90 days. Added key expiry warning in health dashboard.

t6.output: All 21 integration tests pass. Bot deployed with hardened config. Paper trading activated for 48-hour validation period before live switch.

tasks.task: Design model-client.ts abstraction layer for multi-provider LLM access
Implement 4 workloads: gate, extraction, summarization, briefing
Migrate memory-gate.ts from direct Ollama calls to model-client
Run memory benchmark suite to validate no regression

t1.output: Created model-client.ts with OpenAIProvider and OllamaProvider. Auto-selects based on OPENAI_API_KEY availability. Supports temperature, maxTokens, json mode per workload.

t2.output: Implemented 4 workloads with model mapping: gate→qwen2.5:1.5b (Ollama, zero cost), extraction→gpt-4o-mini, summarization→gpt-4o-mini, briefing→gpt-4o-mini. Estimated cost ~$0.13/month.

t3.output: Migrated memory-gate.ts to use mcGenerate() from model-client. Gate still runs on local Ollama (qwen2.5:1.5b) for zero-cost classification. All other memory operations use gpt-4o-mini.

t4.output: ConvoMem: 86.67% (unchanged). LongMemEval: 48.8% → 50% (slight improvement from better answer generation). No regressions detected.

procedural-recall.question: What are the steps in the deploy-production procedure?
procedural-recall.answer: The current version (v2) has 7 steps: (1) tsc --noEmit, (2) run test suite, (3) integration tests against staging DB, (4) push to staging + CI, (5) smoke tests against staging URL, (6) merge staging to main via PR, (7) monitor production logs for 10 minutes

procedural-recall.question: What changed between v1 and v2 of the deploy-production procedure?
procedural-recall.answer: V2 added integration tests against staging DB (step 3), smoke tests against staging URL (step 5 with fallback executor), and increased post-deploy monitoring from 5 to 10 minutes

procedural-recall.question: Which procedure has the highest success rate?
procedural-recall.answer: memory-maintenance has 100% success rate (15 successes, 0 failures)

procedural-recall.question: What is the fallback executor for smoke tests in the deploy-production procedure?
procedural-recall.answer: claude-code is the fallback executor for smoke tests (step 5 of v2)

procedural-recall.question: How many steps does the incident-response procedure have?
procedural-recall.answer: 6 steps: check health, pull error logs, analyze root cause, implement fix, restart service, send incident summary email

procedural-recall.question: Which executor is used most frequently across all procedures?
procedural-recall.answer: claude-code is the most frequently used executor across all procedures

procedural-recall.question: What procedure was used when the blog post 'Reducing Tool Call Failures' was published?
How many times has the backtest-optimization procedure failed?
procedural-recall.answer: Version 1 had 2 failures and version 2 had 0 failures, total 2 failures across all versions

procedural-recall.question: What procedure evolved to add a train/test data split?
procedural-recall.answer: backtest-optimization evolved from v1 to v2, adding a step to split data into train (70%) and test (30%) periods

procedural-recall.question: Which procedures use the hermes executor for their final step?
procedural-recall.answer: deploy-production (v1 and v2) and incident-response use hermes for their final step. Also backtest-optimization v2.

procedural-recall.question: What was the duration of the most recent deploy-production episode?
procedural-recall.answer: 342 seconds (5.7 minutes) for the zouroboros v1.1.0 deploy (episode ep-001)

procedural-recall.question: List all procedures that have never failed
What procedure should be used to handle a production service outage?
procedural-recall.answer: incident-response — steps: check health, pull error logs, analyze root cause, implement fix, restart service, send summary email

procedural-recall.question: How did the backtest-optimization procedure improve its reliability?
procedural-recall.answer: V2 added out-of-sample validation by splitting data into train/test sets, plus an email reporting step. Success rate improved from 78% (7/9) to 100% (9/9)

procedural-recall.question: What is the timeout for the longest step across all procedures?
procedural-recall.answer: 900 seconds (15 minutes) — the post-deploy monitoring step in deploy-production v2

cross-persona-transfer.question: As junior-dev, what does the backend-architect know about swarm routing?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: 6-signal composite routing: capability, health, complexity, history, procedure, temporal

cross-persona-transfer.question: As junior-dev, what deployment conventions does alaric follow?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: All changes to main branch require PR + CI gate — no direct pushes

cross-persona-transfer.question: As alaric, can you access the security auditor's finding about exposed API keys?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: Yes — found exposed API key in client bundle during 2026-03-30 audit, remediated same day

cross-persona-transfer.question: As content-strategist, what is the Stochastic RSI Sharpe ratio?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: The content-strategist cannot access financial-advisor's facts — they are in different pools with no inheritance

cross-persona-transfer.question: As security-auditor, what is the current zouroboros version?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: v1.1.0 released 2026-04-01 — accessible via shared ops-pool membership with alaric

cross-persona-transfer.question: As backend-architect, what are the blog aesthetics conventions?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: The backend-architect cannot directly access content-strategist's facts — no shared pool or inheritance chain

cross-persona-transfer.question: As alaric, what transport types does the swarm support?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: Three transport types: BridgeTransport (Hermes), ACPTransport (Claude Code/Codex), CloudTransport (Gemini) — accessible via shared dev-pool

cross-persona-transfer.question: As junior-dev, what is the position sizing convention for trading?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: junior-dev cannot access financial-advisor's facts — no pool or inheritance link

cross-persona-transfer.question: As security-auditor, what DAG execution modes does the swarm support?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: Streaming and waves modes — accessible via shared dev-pool with backend-architect

cross-persona-transfer.question: As financial-advisor, what secret storage convention does the security auditor enforce?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: financial-advisor cannot access security-auditor's facts — no shared pool (finance-pool vs ops-pool/dev-pool)

cross-persona-transfer.question: As alaric, what are all facts accessible about deployment?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: Alaric can access: own deploy/ci-gate fact (all changes require PR + CI gate), security-auditor's deploy/branch-protection fact (main branch protected, via ops-pool), and related deploy episodes/swarm tasks

cross-persona-transfer.question: As junior-dev, which personas can you inherit knowledge from?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: backend-architect and alaric (via direct inheritance), plus anyone in dev-pool (backend-architect, alaric, security-auditor via transitive)

cross-persona-transfer.question: As content-strategist, what marketing knowledge is available in the marketing pool?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: Content strategist's own facts: blog pipeline (3-persona chain), blog aesthetics, FFB brand voice

cross-persona-transfer.question: As alaric, what does the financial advisor know about crypto trading baselines?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: Alaric cannot access financial-advisor's facts — no shared pool (ops-pool/dev-pool vs finance-pool)

cross-persona-transfer.question: As security-auditor, what conventions does alaric follow for environment variables?
cross-persona-transfer.answer: update_user_service env_vars is full REPLACE not merge — always pass complete set. Accessible via shared ops-pool.

swarm-context-propagation.question: In the site rebuild DAG, what security issues did t2 find that informed t4's implementation?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: t2 found Content-Security-Policy header missing (medium), outdated jQuery 3.5.1 (low), missing X-Frame-Options (low). t4 used these findings to create secure page templates.

swarm-context-propagation.question: In the trading hardening DAG, what did t1's risk review flag that t3 needed to fix?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: t1 flagged VWAP MR missing stop-loss on 2 symbols and Hybrid position sizing exceeding 5% rule on DOGE. t3 implemented 3-tier stop-loss cascade for all strategies.

swarm-context-propagation.question: Which tasks in the site rebuild DAG could run in parallel (wave 1)?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: t1 (audit site structure) and t2 (security scan) — they have no dependencies

swarm-context-propagation.question: What was the critical path in the trading hardening DAG?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: t1 → t3 → t6 (risk review → stop-loss implementation → deploy), or t2 → t4 → t6 (code audit → health gates → deploy), or t2 → t5 → t6 (code audit → security verify → deploy). All three paths converge at t6.

swarm-context-propagation.question: In the memory migration DAG, what abstraction did t1 produce that t2 consumed?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: t1 produced model-client.ts with OpenAIProvider and OllamaProvider abstractions. t2 used it to implement 4 workloads (gate, extraction, summarization, briefing) with per-workload model mapping.

swarm-context-propagation.question: What artifacts were produced in wave 1 of the site rebuild DAG?
How many total artifacts were produced across the entire trading hardening DAG?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: 7 artifacts: risk-review.md, audit-findings.json, stop-loss-impl.ts, health-gates.ts, security-config.json, test-results.json, deploy-log.txt (t6 produced 2)

swarm-context-propagation.question: In the memory migration DAG, did the benchmark validation in t4 show any regression?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: No regression — ConvoMem stayed at 86.67%, LongMemEval improved slightly from 48.8% to 50%

swarm-context-propagation.question: Which persona was responsible for the final deployment step in each DAG?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: alaric in all three DAGs: t6 in site-rebuild, t6 in trading-hardening, t4 in memory-migration (benchmark validation was the final gate)

swarm-context-propagation.question: In the trading hardening DAG, what specific bug did t2 find in the Sharpe calculation?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: t2 found that Sharpe ratio calculation used arithmetic returns instead of log returns

swarm-context-propagation.question: What was the SEO score improvement after the site rebuild DAG completed?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: SEO score improved to 89 (Lighthouse report from t6). Product pages keyword density improved from 0.8% to 2.1% (from t5).

swarm-context-propagation.question: In the memory migration DAG, which workload still runs on local Ollama and why?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: The gate workload runs on local Ollama (qwen2.5:1.5b) for zero-cost classification. All other workloads (extraction, summarization, briefing) use gpt-4o-mini.

swarm-context-propagation.question: What is the estimated monthly cost of the memory model migration?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: Approximately $0.13/month — stated in t2's output about workload implementation

swarm-context-propagation.question: In the trading hardening DAG, what validation period was set before going live?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: 48-hour paper trading validation period before live switch (stated in t6's deploy output)

swarm-context-propagation.question: Which DAG had all tasks succeed on the first attempt?
swarm-context-propagation.answer: All three DAGs had 100% task success: site-rebuild (6/6), trading-hardening (6/6), memory-migration (4/4)
